title: APEX Connect 2026 Closes Today: On-Demand Recordings from All 66 Sessions Now Becoming Available — APEXlang Workshops, Connor McDonald '26ai 100% Demo', Simon Hunt Roadmap
summary: May 20, 2026 — the third and final day of APEX Connect 2026 at Heide Park Soltau (Lower Saxony, Germany). The first major Oracle APEX community conference following Oracle APEX 26.1 GA has wrapped: 66 sessions across three days including APEXlang hands-on workshops by Scott Spendolini, AI Agents demonstrations, Data Reporter previews, and Workflow Parallel Execution deep dives. DOAG confirmed: on-demand video access to all sessions becomes available after conference close (May 20) and remains accessible for at least one year. Priority recordings for enterprise teams: Carsten Czarski keynote 'Low-code with APEX 2026: The State of Play'; Connor McDonald 'The Innovation Continues: More 26ai features, More 100% demo, Zero Slides' (Day 2, May 19); Simon Hunt (Oracle APEX Head of Product Management) — official post-26.1 roadmap and team priorities for 2026–2027; Karen Cannell 'Seriously SELECT AI'. APEX Connect 2026 co-located with DOAG Datenbank + Cloud Infrastructure and CloudLand 2026 (May 18–22, same venue). APEXREST note: on-demand recordings arrive at the optimal moment — OCI rollout of APEX 26.1 is actively progressing to all 50 Oracle Cloud regions, and APEX teams can review new capabilities while preparing for the automatic cloud update. Simon Hunt's roadmap session will serve as the definitive public reference for CTOs and architects planning Oracle APEX through 2026–2028.
sourceName: DOAG APEX Connect 2026 on demand

title: EU 20th Russia Sanctions Package: New Ban on Cybersecurity Services to Russian Entities, 120 New Listings — Compliance Analysis Published May 2026
summary: The EU Council adopted its 20th package of sanctions against Russia on April 23, 2026 — the largest by volume of individual listings in two years, with 120 new designations. Key technology-related measures: a new explicit ban on providing cybersecurity services to the Russian government or legal entities registered in Russia; tightened export restrictions on dual-use technology; 60 new entities added to the military-industrial complex support list, including 28 companies in China, Hong Kong, Türkiye, Thailand, and the UAE. Leading law firms (Skadden, Global Trade & Sanctions Law) published compliance analysis in April–May 2026: companies providing IT services using software or supply chains connected to Russia face material compliance exposure under the 20th package. For businesses in Ukraine: the EU sanctions framework now directly complements domestic 1C/BAS prohibitions (NSDC sanctions, SSSCIP list, Draft Law No. 13505), creating a multi-layered risk environment. APEXREST note: each successive EU sanctions package adds new technology and cybersecurity prohibitions, progressively narrowing any viable legal space for companies with Russian software dependencies. Oracle APEX with Oracle AI Database 26ai is a fully EU-compliant enterprise stack with zero dependency on sanctioned jurisdictions or vendors.
sourceName: EU Council — 20th Package of Sanctions Against Russia

title: Oracle APEX 26.1 OCI Cloud Rollout Underway: Automatic Deployment Across All 50 Oracle Cloud Regions — APEX Service and Autonomous Database Update Without Manual Intervention
summary: Following Oracle APEX 26.1 GA for on-premises (May 15, 2026), the cloud rollout across all 50 Oracle Cloud Infrastructure regions is now underway. OCI rollout timeline: 2–5 weeks from the GA date, covering Autonomous Database Serverless and APEX Service simultaneously across all regions. For teams on OCI: updates apply automatically without manual action; Oracle recommends cloning the database and validating existing applications on the new APEX version before the automatic production update. What APEX 26.1 on OCI delivers: APEXlang (text-based application specification, native git workflow), AI Agents with AI Tools (governance-first agentic AI), AI Interactive Reports (natural language queries in reports without SQL), Data Reporter (self-service BI for business users), Universal Theme Iris, Workflow Parallel Execution. OCI Free Tier allows deploying a full APEX 26.1 + Oracle AI Database 26ai environment at zero licensing cost. APEXREST note: for teams already on Autonomous Database, APEXlang and AI Agents arrive automatically with zero migration effort — the zero-friction path to the APEX 26.1 DevOps and AI stack. Enterprise APEX teams can use the APEX Connect 2026 on-demand recordings to prepare while the cloud update reaches their region.
sourceName: Oracle APEX — Service Updates for APEX on Oracle Cloud

title: First Community Upgrade Reports: Oracle APEX 24.2 → 26.1 — Full Business Logic Compatibility, New UI, APEXlang DevOps Pipeline Setup
summary: In the days following Oracle APEX 26.1 GA (May 15, 2026), the Oracle APEX community is publishing first-hand upgrade reports from the 24.2 → 26.1 path. Yakshaving and other Oracle bloggers document the experience: on-premises APEX 24.2 to 26.1 upgrade takes 1 to several hours depending on application volume and server performance; complete business logic compatibility — existing PL/SQL packages, REST APIs, and page logic carry forward unchanged; APEX 26.1 components are immediately accessible after upgrade without any mandatory migration of existing applications: APEXlang View in Page Designer (read-only inspect), updated Universal Theme Iris, new Workflow APIs. Additional steps for DevOps pipeline readiness: configuring Static IDs for existing components (recommended for stable APEXlang git diffs); setting up VS Code SQLcl APEXlang validation in CI/CD. APEXREST note: the absence of breaking changes in the 24.2 → 26.1 upgrade confirms Oracle APEX preserves production-level backward compatibility — a critical factor for enterprise teams with large APEX application portfolios. Upgrading to 26.1 unlocks APEXlang and AI Agents without rewriting a single line of existing code.
sourceName: Blog in Isolation — APEX 26.1 upgrade from 24.2

title: APEX Connect 2026 Day 2: Connor McDonald — 'The Innovation Continues: More 26ai Features, More 100% Demo, Zero Slides' — Keynote at 09:00 Today
summary: May 19, 2026 (Day 2, APEX Connect 2026, Heide Park Soltau) — Connor McDonald opens the day with his keynote 'The Innovation Continues: More 26ai features, More 100% demo, Zero Slides' at 09:00. Format: 0% slides, 100% live demo — a continuation of Connor McDonald's trademark style for maximum practical depth without marketing noise. The session covers new capabilities in Oracle AI Database 26ai and Oracle APEX 26.1: AI features in APEX components (Interactive Reports, Map Regions, Dynamic Actions), REST integrations, PL/SQL APIs, and new functions for everyday development — alongside AI topics, Connor showcases lesser-known but powerful platform features. Day 2 also includes: Arne Hattendorf and Nina Brötje — 'Let the show begin! — SQL, PL/SQL and APEX at Speed'; multiple workshops and technical sessions across the 66-session program. On-demand video access to all sessions is confirmed by DOAG after the conference closes (after May 20). sourceName: DOAG APEX Connect 2026 — Agenda

title: Oracle Deep Data Security Now GA in Oracle AI Database 26ai: Identity-Aware Database-Level Protection for AI Agents — Row/Column Access Control Against Prompt Injection
summary: Oracle announced the general availability of Oracle Deep Data Security — a new security subsystem in Oracle AI Database 26ai that delivers identity-aware, context-aware access control at the database layer for agentic AI applications, analytics, and enterprise workloads. The problem it solves: an AI agent acting on behalf of a user can, through misconfiguration or prompt injection, attempt to access data belonging to other users. Deep Data Security eliminates this through declarative SQL policies enforced in the database engine itself: user identity and execution context are propagated to the database at runtime; policies define what is visible at row, column, or cell level for a given request regardless of who generates the query — application, agent, or raw SQL; an agent cannot access unauthorized data even if it generates an incorrect or malicious SQL statement. Technical architecture: policy execution happens inside Oracle Database — not in middleware or the application layer — eliminating the need to duplicate filtering logic in each application or agent. Availability: included in the current Release Update of Oracle AI Database 26ai for both on-premises and OCI deployments. APEXREST note: Oracle Deep Data Security is a foundational requirement for moving Oracle APEX AI Agents from pilot to production in regulated environments. For enterprises migrating from legacy ERP: database-level authorization enforcement meets data protection requirements for financial and personal data without additional middleware and without trusting the LLM as a security boundary.
sourceName: Oracle Database Blog — Oracle Deep Data Security Is Now Available in Oracle AI Database 26ai

title: Oracle APEX Data Reporter in APEX 26.1: Self-Service BI for Business Users Without SQL — A Standalone Module Alongside App Builder and SQL Workshop
summary: Oracle published a detailed overview of Oracle APEX Data Reporter — a new standalone module in APEX 26.1 (GA May 15, 2026), positioned alongside App Builder and SQL Workshop in the APEX workspace. Data Reporter allows business users to create and explore reports independently: without writing SQL, without opening Page Designer, without APEX development expertise. Architecture: administrators define trusted datasets — curated collections of tables and views at the workspace level — and control access; business users (Editors) build reporting applications through a streamlined self-service flow: selecting a data source, configuring columns, applying filters, and choosing a layout; APEX Assistant generates SQL automatically from a plain-language description. Governance: business users see only data included in their authorized dataset, not the full database schema. Official documentation: Oracle APEX Data Reporter Guide Release 26.1, published May 2026. APEXREST note: for enterprises migrating from legacy ERP: Data Reporter restores the ability of accountants and analysts to self-build their own reports — as was possible in legacy systems — but with enterprise governance and without unauthorized data access risk. For CTOs: Data Reporter shifts routine reporting workload from APEX developers to business users, freeing development capacity for application delivery.
sourceName: Oracle APEX Blog — Oracle APEX Data Reporter: Putting Self-Service Reporting Back in the Hands of Business Users

title: Red-Teaming Oracle APEX 26.1 AI Agents: First Public Security Analysis 72 Hours After GA — Claude Refused 7 of 10 Attacks on Its Own
summary: Within 72 hours of Oracle APEX 26.1 going GA, an independent researcher published the results of red-team testing the new Oracle APEX AI Agents + Tools capability. Methodology: local APEX 26.1 deployment with an AI Agent connected to Anthropic Claude; deliberately weak system prompt, zero additional security configuration, and a permissive tool set. Results across 10 attack patterns: Claude refused 7 of 10 attacks on its own without any additional configuration — the APEX 26.1 governance model restricts the LLM to calling only tools attached to the agent; tool execution happens within the APEX application boundary; tool output returns to the model before the final response is composed. The 3 patterns that worked require protection at the level of Oracle Tool definition configuration — not at the model level. The researcher published a reproducible build log and a hardening checklist on GitHub. Conclusion: APEX 26.1 AI Agents have built-in protection against most prompt injection attacks by default — but Tool permissions configuration remains the APEX developer's responsibility. APEXREST note: this public red-team confirms that Oracle's governance-first architecture is not declarative — Claude as the AI provider adds an independent security layer. For APEX teams building production AI Agents: the published hardening checklist is a practical starting point for a security review before deployment.
sourceName: DEV Community — Red-teaming Oracle APEX 26.1 AI Agents: Claude refused 7 of 10 attacks

title: Ukraine Draft Law No. 13505: Up to 2% Annual Turnover Fine for Using 1C/BAS — IT Ukraine Association Officially Supports the Bill
summary: The Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine is deliberating on draft law No. 13505 'On the Prohibition of the Use and Distribution of Hostile Software Products and Hostile Information Technology Tools'. Key provisions: statutory definition of 'hostile software' — products from aggressor-state companies or entities on sanctions lists (1C and BAS are explicitly covered); financial penalty of up to 2% of annual turnover for any enterprise found using prohibited software; exclusion from public procurement through the Prozorro system for non-compliant companies; the law applies to actual operational use, not only public procurement. The Verkhovna Rada Committee on Digital Transformation recommended adoption in October 2025. IT Ukraine Association formally endorsed the bill and called for accelerated passage. Concurrently, existing NSDC sanctions against 1C/BAS are in force and the SSSCIP prohibited software list is already published. APEXREST note: Draft Law No. 13505 moves the 1C/BAS issue from regulatory recommendation to direct financial risk — a 2% turnover fine is material even for a mid-sized enterprise. Even before the law passes: active NSDC sanctions and the SSSCIP list already expose non-compliant companies during inspections. Oracle APEX with Oracle AI Database 26ai delivers enterprise-grade infrastructure with zero dependency on sanctioned vendors.
sourceName: Digital Business Services — Up to 2% Turnover Fine: Ukraine Moves to Ban Hostile Software

title: OCI DMS Assessment: Oracle Launches Guided Migration Readiness Assessment for Oracle Database to OCI — Automated Compatibility Checks and Step-by-Step Migration Path
summary: Oracle announced the Assessment capability in OCI Database Migration Service (DMS) — a guided tool for evaluating readiness to migrate Oracle Database to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. Key capabilities: Guided Migration Path — DMS analyzes source and target database parameters and recommends the optimal migration type (online/offline, logical/physical) with clear justification; Compatibility Checks — automatic detection of incompatible objects between source and target databases, with documented issues, risk assessment, recommended actions, and automated fixups for supported scenarios; Configuration Validation — detects missing database configurations before migration begins, preventing avoidable obstacles; Test Table Migration — an optional step to validate the full setup with a representative test before committing to the full migration. Supported sources: Oracle Database on-premises, on OCI, and in third-party clouds (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud); supported targets include Oracle Autonomous AI Database, Oracle Base Database Service, and Exadata Database Service. APEXREST note: OCI DMS Assessment is a practical tool for objectively evaluating migration complexity and risk before committing resources — especially valuable for APEX teams planning migration to OCI Autonomous Database as part of a legacy ERP replacement project.
sourceName: Oracle Data Integration Blog — Announcing OCI DMS Assessment: Simplifying Your Database Migration Journey

title: Oracle APEX Public Roadmap Updated After 26.1 GA: Product Team Priorities for Upcoming Releases — Official Online Resource for CTOs and Architects
summary: Oracle updated the public Oracle APEX Product Roadmap at apex.oracle.com/roadmap following the APEX 26.1 GA release (May 15, 2026). The public roadmap surfaces confirmed platform development directions and features in 'In Progress' or 'Planned' status. Key roadmap categories: AI & Generative AI — expanded AI Tools framework, additional AI providers, AI integration in more APEX components; APEXlang — broader component coverage and full application-level import/export; Workflow — advanced workflow patterns and external system integrations; Security & Compliance — new security controls for enterprise deployments; Developer Experience and App Builder UI — continued code-first workflow enhancements. Simon Hunt (Head of Product Management, Oracle APEX) presents the official post-26.1 roadmap at APEX Connect 2026 (session today, May 18). APEXREST note: Oracle's public APEX product roadmap is the official document for confirmed platform direction — accessible without registration. For CTOs and enterprise architects planning Oracle APEX projects on a 2026–2028 horizon, the roadmap is the primary reference for technical planning and vendor risk assessment.
sourceName: Oracle APEX Product Roadmap

title: OCI Managed MCP Service for Oracle AI Database: Enterprise-Grade Agentic Access to Oracle Databases — OCI Identity, Governed Toolsets, SQL Reports at Zero Additional Cost
summary: Oracle announced OCI Managed MCP Service for Oracle AI Database — a cloud-native, HTTPS-based solution for connecting AI agents and assistants (Claude Desktop, VS Code Copilot, Microsoft Copilot, etc.) to Oracle AI Database 26ai and Oracle Database 19c running on OCI, Oracle AI Database@AWS, @Azure, and @Google Cloud. Architecture highlights: AI agents authenticate through OCI Identity (OAuth 2.0, federation with Microsoft Entra ID and other enterprise IdPs) and access only approved toolsets; SQL Reports allow teams to publish governed, parameterized SQL as reusable MCP-accessible tools — business users get validated natural-language answers without accessing the full database schema; full audit trail via V$SESSION.MODULE (MCP client), V$SESSION.ACTION (LLM name), and the DBTOOLS$MCP_LOG table recording all interactions and SQL executions. Zero additional cost: OCI Managed MCP Service is included in the OCI Database Tools service. Key distinction from SQLcl MCP Server: the previous implementation was client-side for developers; this is an OCI-level managed enterprise service for business users. APEXREST note: for Oracle APEX applications, the MCP Service opens AI-agent access to Oracle business data for end users through any MCP-compatible client — with full Oracle APEX and Oracle Database governance preserved, no ETL pipeline required.
sourceName: Oracle Database Blog — Gain Agentic Access to Oracle Database in the Cloud with Managed MCP Servers

title: Oracle APEX in Oracle's Multicloud Strategy 2026: APEX@Azure, APEX@AWS, and APEX@Google Cloud — Same Code, Same AI Capabilities, Any Cloud Provider
summary: Oracle published a detailed overview of Oracle APEX deployment as part of its 2026 multicloud strategy. Core position: Oracle APEX runs natively on Oracle Database with no application code changes regardless of deployment environment — OCI, Oracle Database@Azure, Oracle Database@AWS, or Oracle Database@Google Cloud. Practical implications: moving an APEX application from on-premises or OCI to a multicloud environment requires no business logic refactoring; Oracle APEX carries no additional licensing cost — if you have Oracle Database, you have APEX; accelerated 2026 regional expansion covers all three major cloud providers with zero-ETL analytics capabilities and unified billing; Oracle APEX is available in 39 Oracle cloud regions across 21 countries. APEX 26.1 GA (May 15, 2026) adds APEXlang and AI Agents, both functioning identically across all Oracle multicloud environments. APEXREST note: for enterprises with heterogeneous IT infrastructure, Oracle's multicloud strategy eliminates vendor lock-in — an APEX application lives wherever Oracle Database lives, regardless of cloud provider. For companies replacing legacy ERP: the Oracle APEX investment is truly portable across clouds with no architectural rework if cloud strategy evolves.
sourceName: Oracle APEX Blog — Oracle APEX in Oracle's Multicloud Database Strategy

title: Ukraine ERP Market Comparative Analysis 2026: Oracle APEX vs SAP B1, Odoo, and IT-Enterprise for Complex Enterprise Requirements
summary: Digital Business Services (DBS) published a detailed comparative analysis of ERP systems for the Ukrainian market in 2026 amid active enforcement of the 1C/BAS ban. Market segmentation by complexity: small businesses — Ukrainian SaaS solutions (Dilovod, Kubera) with native VAT and payroll support; mid-market with international operations — Odoo (flexible open-source architecture with Ukrainian localization) or SAP Business One; large enterprises with non-standard business processes (manufacturing, holding structures, agribusiness, banking) — IT-Enterprise as a domestic enterprise platform, or Oracle APEX as a custom ERP platform with the full Oracle AI Database 26ai stack. Key analyst finding: the challenge is not a lack of 1C alternatives, but that most packaged solutions do not cover vertical market specifics — enterprises with complex processes choose between IT-Enterprise and custom platforms. Oracle APEX in this context is not simply a 1C replacement — it is a foundation for building a purpose-built ERP with Oracle enterprise standards and native AI, with no licensing restrictions on functionality. APEXREST note: for enterprises with non-standard requirements, Oracle APEX with Oracle AI Database 26ai enables building a precise, business-specific solution without packaged-product compromises — complete with APEXlang DevOps stack and production-ready AI Agents.
sourceName: Digital Business Services — Comparative Analysis of ERP Systems in Ukraine 2026

Oracle OCI is winning AI workload contracts through: lower GPU compute costs compared to AWS and Azure on equivalent AI configurations; vertical integration of Oracle AI Database 26ai + Oracle APEX + OCI GenAI as a single platform with no third-party dependencies; confirmed IL7 security clearance for defense-grade classified environments. In parallel, the OCI regional network and Alloy sovereign cloud footprint continue to expand — SoftBank Oracle Alloy Japan is the production-reference architecture for regulated industries. APEXREST note: for CTOs and architects making platform decisions on a 3–5 year horizon: Oracle OCI + APEX + AI Database 26ai is a strategically stable choice backed by confirmed enterprise security and continuously expanding AI capacity. APEX projects launched today will have access to growing AI resources without architectural changes as OCI capacity increases.
sourceName: ERP Today — Oracle Loads Up on AI Infrastructure as OCI Backlog, Data Center Commitments Surge

title: OCI Compute with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell: Now Generally Available for Enterprise Multimodal AI — 8 GPUs × 96 GB GDDR7, 3 TB RAM, $4.50/GPU-hr
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure announced general availability of the new OCI Compute shape with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs and Intel Xeon 6. Key specifications: 8 NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs with 96 GB GDDR7 each (768 GB total GPU memory); 3 TB of system RAM — up to 2× more than comparable hyperscaler offerings; 61.44 TB of local NVMe storage — up to 4× greater than competing configurations; networking up to 400 Gbps front-end and 1600 Gbps RDMA back-end. Pricing: $4.50/GPU-hour. The platform unifies AI acceleration, simulation, and visual computing in a single solution — the first OCI shape purpose-built for true multimodal AI workloads processing video, images, text, and audio within a single system. Oracle Acceleron SmartNIC provides line-rate encryption, zero-downtime patching, and NVMe storage acceleration at the NIC layer. APEXREST note: for enterprise Oracle APEX applications requiring multimodal AI analysis — documents, images, video, and audio alongside structured Oracle business data — OCI RTX PRO opens new capabilities without leaving the Oracle infrastructure stack or requiring separate external ML platforms.
sourceName: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Blog — Announcing GA of OCI Compute with RTX PRO

title: Oracle APEX 26.1 Post-GA: Workflow Parallel Execution, Universal Theme Iris, and a Redesigned App Builder — Technical Deep Dive into New Platform Capabilities
summary: Following the Oracle APEX 26.1 GA (May 15), Oracle published the full detailed list of new platform capabilities. Key technical updates beyond the headlining features (APEXlang, AI Agents, AI Interactive Reports): Workflow Parallel Execution — APEX Workflow now supports parallel task execution, extended Workflow APIs, and tenant-aware workflow behavior for multi-tenant applications; Universal Theme Iris — new Oracle-branded style with a refreshed palette, enhanced accessibility, and Font APEX 2.5; Template Components — client-side partials, Template Directives supporting item, region, list, and page templates, autocomplete in the editor; redesigned App Builder UI — a cleaner, more modern Page Designer, updated navigation, dialogs, and workspace sorting by recent login. Additionally: the official APEX 26.1 documentation has been published on docs.oracle.com and included in Oracle Help Center. APEXREST note: Workflow Parallel Execution in 26.1 is a material upgrade for enterprise APEX projects with parallel business processes — multi-step approvals, parallel document workflows — natively, without external BPM systems or additional licensing.
sourceName: Oracle APEX 26.1 — New Features Documentation

title: Ukraine SSSCIP: Prohibited Software List Covers 20+ 1C Products — BAS Officially Banned for State Sector and Critical Infrastructure
summary: Ukraine's State Service of Special Communications and Information Protection (SSSCIP) published the current list of prohibited software. The list includes more than 20 products from the 1C company: 1C:Enterprise, BAS (Business Automation Software), 1C:Accounting, 1C:Trade and Warehouse, 1C:Manufacturing Enterprise Management, and other editions. The ban covers state sector and critical infrastructure entities and applies to actual operational use, not only public procurement. Surveys indicate over 60% of Ukrainian companies still use 1C, including some government institutions. Under Ukrainian law, legal responsibility for data accuracy rests with the company's manager — making migration not merely a technical decision but also a matter of personal executive accountability. APEXREST note: the SSSCIP list removes any ambiguity about the scope of the ban — BAS/1C is officially prohibited. Deadlines: critical infrastructure — December 1, 2026; state sector — September 1, 2026. Oracle APEX with Oracle AI Database 26ai delivers an enterprise-grade platform certified for secure environments, with no dependency on sanctioned software vendors.
sourceName: SSSCIP Ukraine — List of Prohibited Software

title: Oracle APEX 26.1 Is Now Generally Available: APEXlang, AI Agents, and AI Interactive Reports — The Largest Platform Release of 2026, Three Days Before APEX Connect
summary: Oracle announced the general availability of Oracle APEX 26.1 — the most significant platform release of 2026. Three major new capabilities: APEXlang is now officially GA (no longer Early Adopter only) — an open, declarative, human-readable specification language for Oracle APEX applications expressed as structured .apx text files, supporting human-readable git diffs, SQLcl validation, VS Code integration, and an APEXlang View in Page Designer for real-time metadata inspection; AI Agents with AI Tools — a new native architecture for adding AI agents to APEX applications: the AI Agent manages the conversation, AI Tools define what the agent is authorized to do, execution stays within the APEX application boundary with full governance and audit trail; AI Interactive Reports — conversational BI built into standard APEX, users query reports in natural language ('European customers grouped by industry', 'highlight top customers') without writing SQL. APEX 26.1 also includes significant enhancements to workflow automation, security, translation management, maps, and Universal Theme. The release is available today for on-premises deployment across all Oracle AI Database distributions (Exadata, Linux, Windows); rollout to OCI regions and Oracle multicloud environments is planned for the coming weeks. APEXREST note: APEX 26.1 GA marks the shift from AI as a feature to AI as a native component of the platform. For enterprises evaluating Oracle APEX as a replacement for legacy ERP: APEX 26.1 with APEXlang and AI Agents delivers enterprise low-code with production-ready AI automation and a full DevOps stack in a single product.
sourceName: Oracle APEX Blog — Announcing Oracle APEX 26.1 General Availability

title: APEXlang Now in Production: APEXlang View in Page Designer, SQLcl Validation, Oracle SQL Developer for VS Code — Oracle APEX Gets an Official Enterprise DevOps Stack
summary: With the release of Oracle APEX 26.1 GA, APEXlang moves from Early Adopter preview status to a fully supported, documented GA feature with an official toolchain. What is available for developers in GA: APEXlang View in Page Designer — real-time inspection of page metadata without leaving App Builder; Static IDs for meaningful git diffs — each component receives a stable identifier, making version comparisons human-readable for the whole team; SQLcl validation of APEXlang source before import — command-line checking integrates cleanly into CI/CD pipelines; Oracle SQL Developer for VS Code — full APEXlang support in the IDE, enabling AI agents to generate and modify .apx files directly. Michael Hichwa (SVP Oracle Software Development) described the concept as 'capture of intent, not implementation' — APEXlang stores developer intent in a structured format that both humans and AI agents understand. APEXlang transforms Oracle APEX from a GUI-only tool into a platform with a complete code-first developer workflow: git, code review, CI/CD, and AI-assisted development become native, not workarounds. APEXREST note: for enterprise teams building an ERP replacement on Oracle APEX, APEXlang in GA means new projects start with a production DevOps-ready architecture from day one — no unreadable binary XML exports, no workarounds for code review.
sourceName: Oracle APEX 26.1 — What's New

title: Oracle Agentic AI Strategy 2026: From Fusion AI Agents to Oracle APEX AI Agents — A Unified Governance-First AI Automation Ecosystem for Enterprise
summary: Alongside the Oracle APEX 26.1 GA release, Oracle published a detailed overview of its unified agentic AI strategy — spanning from Fusion Agentic Applications to Oracle APEX AI Agents. Oracle's core position: agentic AI must be embedded in the business system rather than existing as a standalone chatbot — agents access only approved actions through AI Tools, execution stays within the application boundary, every action is captured in a full audit trail. Three tiers of Oracle agentic AI in 2026: Oracle Fusion AI Agents — embedded inside Fusion ERP/HCM/SCM for Oracle Fusion customers; Oracle APEX AI Agents — native agentic AI architecture in custom APEX applications for any business process (now GA in 26.1); OCI Generative AI Agents — managed platform for RAG and multi-agent workflows in the OCI ecosystem. Shared governance-first principle: an agent acts only within its explicitly authorized scope, which directly addresses the core enterprise objection to AI automation ('we cannot trust AI with our business data'). APEXREST note: for companies replacing legacy ERP with Oracle APEX, APEX AI Agents in 26.1 enable building intelligent automation for any business process — purchase orders, approvals, document handling — within the same APEX platform, without external AI tooling and without losing governance and control.
sourceName: Oracle AI & Data Science Blog — Agentic AI in the Enterprise: How Oracle Is Powering the Next Wave

title: APEX Connect 2026 in 4 Days: Final Schedule Confirmed, On-Demand Registration Open — APEXlang, SELECT AI, and the Official Oracle APEX Roadmap May 18–20
summary: APEX Connect 2026 opens in 4 days (May 18–20, Heide Park Soltau, Lower Saxony, Germany). DOAG confirmed the final schedule and opened on-demand registration for remote attendees. Priority sessions for enterprise teams: Carsten Czarski — keynote on APEXlang and Oracle APEX 26.1 (the first detailed public unveiling of the new declarative format for APEX applications); Simon Hunt (Oracle APEX Product Management) — official APEX product roadmap and team priorities for 2026–2027; Karen Cannell — practical workshop 'Seriously SELECT AI' with real-world business use cases; joint sessions with DOAG Datenbank + Cloud Infrastructure and CloudLand 2026 (three conferences on one venue, May 18–22). On-demand video and slides from all sessions will be available after the event for teams unable to attend in person. For CTOs and architects making platform decisions in 2026–2027: APEX Connect 2026 is the first public event where Oracle will fully reveal APEXlang and confirm the Oracle APEX 26.1 GA timeline.
sourceName: DOAG APEX Connect 2026

title: Oracle AI Database 26ai On-Premises for Linux x86-64: Community Publishes Practical Deployment Guides — Vector Search, SQL Firewall, and True Cache Without OCI
summary: Following the GA of Oracle AI Database 26ai for Linux x86-64 on-premises (January 27, 2026, RU 23.26.1), the Oracle community — Virtual-DBA, Mike Dietrich, and ORACLE-BASE — is actively publishing practical deployment and migration guides in May 2026. Key facts: Oracle AI Database 26ai is available for download at oracle.com — Enterprise Edition for Linux x86-64 (Windows, AIX, and other platforms planned for later in 2026); direct upgrade from Oracle 19c or patch-only transition from 23ai — no database reinstallation or application recertification required; what is included on-premises: AI Vector Search (VECTOR data type, DBMS_VECTOR), built-in SQL Firewall, True Cache, JSON Relational Duality, Post-Quantum encryption (ML-KEM), RAFT-based distributed replication, Apache Iceberg Lakehouse support; Oracle AI Database 26ai is the LTS release replacing Oracle Database 23ai. For Oracle APEX on-premises teams: upgrading the database to 26ai immediately unlocks SELECT AI, AI Vector Search, and all AI features in APEX 24.2 without moving to the cloud. APEXREST note: for enterprises not yet ready for the cloud, Oracle AI Database 26ai on-premises delivers the full AI stack without an OCI dependency, with a direct upgrade path from 19c.
sourceName: Oracle Database Blog — GA of Oracle AI Database 26ai for Linux x86-64 On-Premises

title: IBM and Oracle Expand 40-Year Partnership: IBM watsonx Orchestrate on OCI in July, Red Hat RHEL via Oracle Universal Credits, AI Agents for Enterprise Oracle APEX
summary: IBM and Oracle announced an expansion of their 40-year strategic partnership in May 2026. Key new directions: IBM watsonx Orchestrate (IBM's flagship AI agent portfolio) will be available on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure in July 2026 — multi-agent AI automation for the Oracle ecosystem without changing cloud providers; Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) can now be purchased directly within OCI using Oracle Universal Credits — a streamlined licensing model for enterprise teams; IBM Consulting is expanding managed services for Oracle customers: a new managed service for Maximo on OCI and support for migrating Maximo workloads to Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP; IBM Envizi (ESG data and reporting) launches as SaaS on OCI (first region: Saudi Arabia); IBM Turbonomic has been verified for OCI resource optimization. APEXREST note: the IBM-Oracle integration opens watsonx AI agents as an additional automation layer for Oracle APEX applications within the same OCI environment — watsonx Orchestrate combined with SELECT AI and Oracle AI Database 26ai is a powerful stack for enterprise AI-driven process automation.
sourceName: IBM Newsroom — IBM and Oracle Expand Partnership to Help Organizations Modernize with AI and Cloud

title: Ukraine's 1C/BAS Replacement Market: Structured Overview May 2026 — Dilovod, Odoo, SAP B1, or Oracle APEX? A Framework for Different Business Types
summary: During April–May 2026, multiple IT publications and consulting firms published structured overviews of the 1C/BAS replacement market for Ukrainian businesses. Market segmentation: small businesses and sole proprietors — Dilovod and MASTER as Ukrainian-localized solutions with VAT and payroll support; mid-market with international operations — Odoo (flexible open-source architecture) or SAP Business One; large enterprises with complex processes (manufacturing, holding structures, agribusiness) — Oracle APEX as a custom ERP platform or Oracle Fusion. A separate concern is emerging: some companies are attempting workarounds — purchasing licenses abroad or using unlicensed 1C versions. This carries dual risk: regulatory exposure during inspections under the enforcement framework active from late 2026, and cybersecurity risk from absent patching and vendor support. APEXREST note: for companies with complex business processes that do not fit a standard packaged solution, Oracle APEX is the custom ERP platform with the full Oracle AI Database 26ai stack — no functional licensing restrictions, full customization for industry-specific requirements, and enterprise-grade security.
sourceName: Cityhost — Replacement of 1C and BAS in Ukraine 2026: Overview and Comparison of Alternatives

title: APEXlang: Technical Deep Dive from the Community — Specification-Driven Development and AI-Generated APEX Apps as a Paradigm Shift, Ahead of APEX Connect 2026
summary: Ahead of APEX Connect 2026 (starting May 18 at Heide Park Soltau), leading Oracle bloggers at Traust, MaxAPEX, and Into The Oracle Verse published detailed technical analyses of APEXlang. Key findings: APEXlang is a text-based format (.apx files) replacing the monolithic SQL export — supports human-readable git diff, AI generation, and specification-driven development workflows; Michael Hichwa (SVP Oracle Software Development) defined the core concept: APEXlang captures intent rather than raw implementation code; compiler preview is already in the APEX 26.1 Early Adopter environment with simultaneous App Builder and text editor editing support; bulk changes via search/replace in a text file are now possible — something fundamentally impossible with binary SQL exports. Strategic importance for enterprise teams: APEXlang resolves a practical problem — code review, git-based workflows, and AI-assisted development were difficult to implement with binary XML exports. APEX Connect 2026 will be the first public forum where Oracle details the APEXlang roadmap via Carsten Czarski's keynote. Oracle APEX 26.1 GA is expected in 2026.
sourceName: Traust — APEXlang: How Oracle Is Rebuilding APEX for Gen AI

title: Oracle OCI Cleared for US DoD Classified AI Networks (IL6/IL7): Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Among 8 Companies for Defense-Grade AI — Highest Security Clearance Confirmed
summary: In early May 2026, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure joined eight leading technology companies cleared to deploy AI capabilities on classified U.S. Department of Defense networks. Impact Level 6 (IL6) covers secret networks; IL7 is the highest classification level in the US. Oracle joins AWS, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, OpenAI, SpaceX, and Reflection on the approved list. The agreement enables OCI AI capabilities to be used for intelligence analysis, processing of classified data, and decision support within closed defense environments. Notable context: Anthropic was excluded for insisting on safety guardrails for AI use in military operations — Oracle passed this filter. APEXREST note: IL7 classified network clearance is the highest confirmed security level for any cloud platform worldwide. For enterprises evaluating OCI as an Oracle APEX hosting platform: OCI is certified at the level where US state secrets are stored in the cloud — a fundamentally different security tier compared to any sanctioned legacy ERP vendor. Oracle OCI satisfies DORA, NIS2, ISO 27001/17/18, and FIPS 140-2.
sourceName: Oracle News — Agreement with the Department of War for AI on Classified Cloud Networks

title: SoftBank Oracle Alloy Japan: First Production Sovereign Cloud on OCI Launched April 2026 — 200+ OCI Services Within Local Data Centers, a Reference for Regulated Industries
summary: SoftBank Corp. confirmed the production launch of 'Cloud PF Type A' — the first large-scale sovereign cloud platform on Oracle Alloy — in April 2026 at its eastern Japan data center (western Japan planned for October 2026). Oracle Alloy is Oracle's complete cloud infrastructure platform enabling partners to become cloud service providers with 200+ OCI services deployed within their own data centers. SoftBank pairs its own generative AI models with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure and OCI Enterprise AI: inference, training, and data storage remain exclusively within Japanese jurisdiction without traversing public networks. This is the first large-scale production reference architecture for sovereign AI on Oracle Alloy — full OCI Enterprise AI stack combined with local data residency requirements in a single system. APEXREST note: SoftBank Oracle Alloy confirms that the full OCI stack — Oracle APEX, Oracle AI Database 26ai, OCI Enterprise AI — can be deployed in sovereign mode for any jurisdiction. For regulated industries (banking, critical infrastructure, government): Oracle provides a production-proven reference architecture for sovereign cloud AI.
sourceName: Oracle News — SoftBank Corp. Accelerates Japan's AI and Sovereign Cloud Future with Oracle Alloy

title: Oracle APEX @University Initiative: Oracle Brings AI Low-Code Development to Higher Education — Free Program for Universities and Colleges Worldwide
summary: The Oracle APEX Product Management team, alongside Oracle Academy and Database Learning teams, launched the APEX @University Initiative — a free program integrating Oracle APEX and AI-assisted development into university curricula worldwide. The program includes: structured learning content created by Oracle APEX product team members; faculty support for curriculum design and practical lab sessions; student projects built on Oracle APEX, including real institutional applications such as student portals and academic management systems; a direct feedback channel to the Oracle APEX product team. The University of Tennessee System joined Oracle Academy as one of the first participants in the initiative. APEXREST note: APEX @University means the next generation of developers will encounter low-code enterprise development and AI-assisted APEX programming in university curricula — starting in 2026. For enterprises planning long-term Oracle APEX projects: the talent market for Oracle APEX developers is actively expanding, with academic programs reinforcing enterprise adoption at scale.
sourceName: Oracle APEX Blog — Introducing The Oracle APEX @University Initiative

title: OCI X12 Standard Acceleron with Intel Xeon 6: 2.5× Bare Metal Performance, 120 Cores, 1.15 TB RAM — Next-Generation OCI Infrastructure for AI Workloads
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure announced the launch of OCI X12 Standard Acceleron Compute instances based on Intel Xeon 6 processors. Key specifications: 2.5× higher bare metal performance and ~50% better VM performance compared to the previous-generation X9 Standard (3rd Gen Intel Xeon); bare metal configuration — 120 Intel Xeon 6 cores, 1.15 TB RAM, up to 100 Gbps networking; Oracle Acceleron SmartNIC provides line-rate traffic encryption, zero-downtime network patching, and NVMe-based storage acceleration at the NIC layer. X12 is rolling out across select OCI regions throughout 2026. For Oracle APEX applications on OCI: X12 Standard Acceleron directly accelerates APEX Listener, PL/SQL processing, and SELECT AI calls — particularly impactful for enterprise APEX systems with intensive business process and AI workloads. APEXREST note: the new OCI compute generation means APEX applications in the cloud receive a meaningful performance boost with no code or architecture changes — unlike on-premises legacy ERP, where server upgrades required capital investment and migration projects.
sourceName: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Blog — Announcing OCI X12 Standard Acceleron Compute

title: Oracle AI Database Agent for Gemini Enterprise: Natural Language Access to Oracle Data in Google Workspace Without SQL — Multicloud AI Integration in Preview
summary: At Google Cloud Next 2026, Oracle and Google Cloud announced the preview of Oracle AI Database Agent for Gemini Enterprise in Google Cloud Marketplace. Core capability: business users can query Oracle data in plain language directly through Gemini Enterprise without writing SQL or understanding the database schema — the agent interprets the request and returns an answer grounded in trusted Oracle business data. Example: 'Which products are at risk of stockouts next quarter, and what are the revenue implications?'. Additional capabilities: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) compatibility and Google ADK for automated multi-step workflows; Oracle DB integration with Google Cloud Database Center for centralized management; Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud expanded to 15 regions (20 sites); future integration with BigQuery/BigLake for cross-platform Iceberg data access. APEXREST note: for enterprises with a hybrid stack (Google Workspace + Oracle APEX or Oracle Database), this agent is the first enterprise-grade solution enabling Gemini Enterprise to reason directly over Oracle data without separate ETL integrations or additional BI licenses.
sourceName: Oracle Cloud Blog — Bringing Business Data Insights to Every User with Gemini Enterprise and Oracle AI Database

title: Oracle AI Database 26ai: New Standards for Operational Continuity — Fast Start Reconfiguration, RAC Rolling Update, and Smart Connection Rebalancing
summary: The Oracle DBA community has published a detailed analysis of Oracle AI Database 26ai's operational continuity capabilities. Key new features: Fast Start Reconfiguration — rapid cluster adaptation to infrastructure changes; RAC Two-Stage Rolling Update — staged upgrades without interrupting production workloads; Smart Connection Rebalancing — smooth redistribution of connections during topology changes without dropping client sessions; Pluggable Database Recovery Isolation — failure containment at the individual PDB level without impacting other tenants in a multitenant environment; Rolling Single-Server Maintenance — rolling updates even on a single server reduce operational risk. All features are available on OCI Autonomous Database and Exadata. APEXREST note: for enterprises planning migration from legacy ERP, Oracle AI Database 26ai delivers a level of operational continuity that legacy systems fundamentally cannot match — patching and updates without maintenance windows become the standard, not the exception.
sourceName: TechnoDBA Blog — Oracle AI Database 26ai: Redefining Operational Continuity

title: Oracle Monthly Product Pulse May 2026: ZDM 26.1, GROUP BY ALL in SQL, New Autonomous DB Capabilities — May Digest for Oracle APEX Teams
summary: Oracle ACE published the Monthly Product Pulse for May 2026 covering key Oracle stack technical updates. Oracle Zero Downtime Migration (ZDM) 26.1: new capabilities for migrating databases to Oracle Cloud, multicloud environments, and Exadata with minimal downtime — important for teams planning the transition from on-premises Oracle to OCI. SQL additions in Oracle AI Database 26ai: GROUP BY ALL — automatically groups by all non-aggregated columns in a query (significant simplification for analytical SQL queries); DATEDIFF — new standard date difference function. Autonomous Database Exadata VM Cluster: clusters provisioned after April 7, 2026 support both Oracle 19c and 26ai software simultaneously — a flexible migration path without forcing an immediate jump to 26ai. Expanded ADB observability: new Dynamic Performance Views with access to historical performance metrics and Exadata cell activity. APEXREST note: ZDM 26.1 and dual 19c/26ai support on the same Autonomous Exadata VM Cluster reduce risk and timeline for Oracle APEX projects migrating to the new platform.
sourceName: Oracle ACE Blog — Monthly Product Pulse: May 2026 — Oracle Technical Resources

title: Oracle Launches Monthly Critical Security Patch Updates (CSPU) Starting May 28, 2026 — Critical Vulnerabilities No Longer Wait for the Quarterly Cycle
summary: Oracle announced a fundamental change to its security update cadence: starting May 28, 2026, monthly Critical Security Patch Updates (CSPUs) will ship alongside the existing quarterly CPU. The first CSPU releases May 28; the second June 16; the third August 18. Each CSPU is smaller in scope and focused exclusively on critical vulnerabilities, enabling teams to close them without waiting for the next quarterly release. The driver: Oracle has deployed AI into vulnerability detection and analysis — the volume of identified and resolved issues grew to a point where quarterly releases are no longer sufficient. On OCI, all CSPUs are applied automatically with zero downtime. For on-premises Oracle Database and Oracle APEX: teams must plan a monthly test-and-apply cycle — particularly critical for APEX instances handling financial and personal business data. APEXREST note: the CSPU cadence adds another concrete argument for OCI-hosted Oracle APEX — in the cloud, Oracle applies patches automatically without any IT department overhead.
sourceName: Oracle Security Blog — Monthly Critical Security Patch Updates Begin May 28, 2026

title: Oracle APEX + MCP + AI Agent: Automated Oracle Forms Analysis Generates PL/SQL Package and APEX Specification — Migration Time Drops to 1–2 Days
summary: Oracle published two complementary resources for AI-accelerated Oracle Forms modernization. 'MCP + AI: Helping You Modernize Your Oracle Forms Apps to Oracle APEX' covers using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for Oracle Database: the AI agent gets direct access to the database schema, automatically analyzes form business logic, validates objects in the target schema, and moves logic to Oracle AI Database. 'Transforming Oracle Forms to Oracle APEX in the AI Era: AI Agent' introduces the oracle-forms-migration.agent.md workflow in VS Code: the agent analyzes the Oracle Forms XML file, validates database dependencies, optionally generates a supporting PL/SQL package, and produces a functional specification as a structured output for the APEX developer. Practical impact: form analysis and specification preparation that previously took weeks is now reduced to 1–2 days. For enterprises with Oracle Forms alongside legacy ERP: Oracle now provides a ready-made AI toolkit for both migration tracks within a single Oracle APEX project.
sourceName: Oracle APEX Blog — MCP + AI: Helping You Modernize Your Oracle Forms Apps to Oracle APEX

title: AI-Enabled Cyber Threats Against Oracle Databases: LLM Agents Automate Exploit Development — Unpatched Oracle APEX Instances Face Elevated Risk in 2026
summary: Oracle and independent researchers have documented a new wave of AI-generated cyber threats targeting Oracle Database and connected applications. AI tools — including LLM assistants and automated agent pipelines — have significantly lowered the barrier to developing and launching exploits against known Oracle vulnerabilities. A vulnerability that previously required manual discovery can now be identified by an AI agent in minutes. This is one of the key factors behind Oracle's shift to monthly CSPU (Critical Security Patch Update) releases starting May 28, 2026. For enterprises maintaining on-premises Oracle APEX or Oracle Database without an active patch management program: cyber incident risk in 2026 is materially higher than in 2024. APEXREST note: migration to OCI Autonomous Database automatically resolves this — Oracle applies all CSPUs without downtime or manual action, eliminating the risk of forgotten vulnerabilities in production environments.
sourceName: Mike Dietrich — Patch your databases against AI-enabled cybersecurity threats

title: APEX Connect 2026 in 7 Days: DOAG Confirms On-Demand Video Access — APEXlang, SELECT AI, and the APEX Roadmap for Teams That Cannot Attend in Person
summary: APEX Connect 2026 (May 18–20, Heide Park Soltau, Lower Saxony, Germany) opens in exactly 7 days. DOAG confirmed on-demand video access to all conference sessions — recordings and slides will be available after the event for teams unable to attend in person. On-demand content includes recordings and slides from all sessions, the APEXlang preview by Carsten Czarski, the official APEX Product Roadmap from Simon Hunt (Oracle Product Management), and the practical 'Seriously SELECT AI' session by Karen Cannell. APEX Connect 2026 runs concurrently with DOAG Datenbank + Cloud Infrastructure and CloudLand 2026 — three conferences at the same venue (May 18–22). This conference will be the first public forum where Oracle details APEXlang and the APEX 26.1 roadmap — a key reference point for teams making platform decisions for 2026–2027.
sourceName: DOAG APEX Connect 2026 on demand

title: Oracle APEX 23.2: End of Support on May 31, 2026 — 21 Days Left to Upgrade to APEX 24.2
summary: Oracle has confirmed: support for Oracle APEX 23.2 ends on May 31, 2026 — no further extensions are planned. The support period had already been extended once, from November 2025 to May 2026, to accommodate the delayed releases of APEX 24.1 and 24.2. After May 31, Oracle will not provide security patches, bug fixes, or official support for APEX 23.2. Current support landscape: APEX 24.2 — primary supported release (through July 31, 2027); APEX 24.1 — supported through December 31, 2026; APEX 26.1 Early Adopter is available now. Teams on OCI Autonomous Database receive updates automatically through the OCI update cycle; on-premises instances require a manual upgrade before the deadline. APEX 24.2 includes AI Interactive Reports, SELECT AI without additional configuration, and the improved AI Assistant in App Builder — upgrading from 23.2 to 24.2 does not require refactoring business logic.
sourceName: endoflife.date — Oracle APEX Support Policy

title: OCI Enterprise AI: xAI Grok 4.3 and NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni — Multimodal AI Models Now Available for Oracle APEX SELECT AI
summary: Oracle published its May 2026 'What's New in AI' digest and announced the launch of OCI Enterprise AI — the platform for moving from AI experimentation to production deployments. Two new models are now available on OCI: xAI Grok 4.3 — a reasoning model with strong performance in logic, mathematics, coding, and multi-step analysis; NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni — a fully open-source multimodal model that reasons across video, audio, images, and text in a single system — the first model of this class on OCI. Both are available via OCI Generative AI as AI providers for Oracle APEX SELECT AI and APEX AI Assistant without changes to existing APEX application architecture. For APEX developers: Nemotron 3 Nano Omni enables document analysis with images and audio transcription directly in APEX applications — without separate ML services.
sourceName: Oracle AI & Data Science Blog — What's New in AI: May 2026

title: Oracle AI Database@AWS: 12 AWS Regions Now GA Including Frankfurt, Tokyo, Mumbai, Seoul — Oracle's Multicloud Strategy Goes Global
summary: Oracle and AWS confirmed general availability of Oracle AI Database@AWS across 12 AWS regions: US East (N. Virginia, Ohio), US West (Oregon), Canada Central, Frankfurt, Dublin, London, Tokyo, Sydney, Mumbai, Hyderabad, and Seoul. The service enables running Oracle Exadata and RAC workloads on AWS infrastructure while retaining Oracle licenses and all existing business logic. Alongside this, Oracle and AWS announced direct multicloud network integration — OCI ↔ AWS Interconnect: high-performance connectivity without transit traffic, with the first region in AWS US East (N. Virginia). 20+ additional regions are planned before end of 2026. For enterprises with existing AWS infrastructure: Database@AWS is the bridge between your current AWS stack and Oracle APEX + Oracle AI Database 26ai, without a full migration to OCI.
sourceName: AWS — Oracle Database@AWS Available in Twelve AWS Regions

title: APEXlang Ahead of APEX Connect 2026: Technical Deep Dive into Oracle APEX's New Declarative Language — Git Workflow and AI Code Generation
summary: The Oracle APEX community is actively discussing APEXlang ahead of APEX Connect 2026 (starting May 18 at Heide Park Soltau). APEXlang is a text-based format (YAML/JSON-like) for describing an APEX application: human-readable git diff instead of binary XML exports; AI-friendly format that LLM models can generate or modify directly; support for parallel editing via App Builder and a text editor simultaneously. APEXlang compiler preview is available now in the APEX 26.1 Early Adopter environment. For teams with legacy APEX projects: APEXlang is a path to modern DevOps practices without rewriting existing applications — incremental migration via git at the individual component level. APEX Connect 2026 will be the first public forum where APEXlang is presented in detail with the official product roadmap.
sourceName: Into The Oracle Verse — Oracle APEX 26.1: APEXlang, AI Innovations

title: APEX Connect 2026 — 9 Days to Go: APEXlang Preview, SELECT AI in Practice, and the Official Oracle APEX Roadmap
summary: May 18–20, 2026, Heide Park Soltau (Lower Saxony, Germany) hosts APEX Connect 2026 by DOAG — the largest annual Oracle APEX community conference. Featured keynote: Carsten Czarski 'Low-code with APEX 2026 — AI, APEXlang, new components, and the state of the platform' — the first extended public preview of APEXlang and Oracle APEX 26.1. Karen Cannell will present 'Seriously SELECT AI' — practical SELECT AI applications in Oracle AI Database 26ai. Simon Hunt (Oracle APEX Product Management) delivers the official roadmap update with team priorities. The event co-locates with DOAG Datenbank + Cloud Infrastructure and CloudLand 2026 on the same venue (May 18–22). For teams making platform decisions in 2026: APEX Connect is where Oracle APEX shows where it is heading in 2027.
sourceName: DOAG APEX Connect 2026

title: Oracle APEX AI Interactive Reports: Natural Language Queries in Business Reports — 'Show Top 10 Customers This Quarter' in the Search Bar
summary: The Oracle APEX team published a detailed overview of the new APEX AI Interactive Reports feature. The familiar Interactive Report search bar now accepts natural language queries: 'show top 10 customers this quarter', 'filter orders with Pending status over 30 days', 'compare regional sales for the last 6 months' — APEX automatically translates the request into a SQL filter and displays the result without any code. The feature is available in APEX 24.2+ with Oracle AI Database or OCI Generative AI connected. For business users accustomed to legacy ERP reports: APEX AI Interactive Reports is the first large-scale implementation of 'conversational BI' in a low-code enterprise platform without a separate BI tool.
sourceName: Oracle APEX Blog

title: OCI UAE North: Oracle Launches Generative AI Services in UAE Region — Data Residency Compliance for the Middle East
summary: Oracle announced general availability (GA) of OCI Generative AI Services in the UAE North region (Abu Dhabi). The region is certified for UAE Data Residency requirements and ISO 27001/27017/27018. Available models: Cohere Command R+, Meta Llama 3 (8B and 70B). Oracle APEX + SELECT AI support UAE North as an endpoint for AI queries. For Middle East companies considering migration from on-premises ERP (legacy systems, local SAP variants) to cloud architecture: Oracle now addresses data residency requirements in the UAE region with a full AI services stack.
sourceName: Oracle Cloud Blog

title: Ukraine Enforces Legacy ERP Ban: Cabinet of Ministers Approves Mechanism for Blocking 1C/BAS Licenses in State Sector and Critical Infrastructure
summary: The Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine adopted a resolution detailing the enforcement mechanism for the ban on 1C/BAS in the state sector and critical infrastructure. The State Tax Service and State Service of Special Communications receive authority to add software to the prohibited tools registry and require migration confirmation during scheduled inspections. For commercial enterprises — advisory in nature, but banks and insurance companies are included in the critical infrastructure list. Deadlines: state sector — by September 1, 2026; critical infrastructure — by December 1, 2026. APEXREST comment: the document removes the last uncertainty about timelines and scope — migration projects started now fit comfortably within regulatory deadlines.
sourceName: Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine

title: Fusion Agentic Applications: Oracle Embeds AI Agents into ERP/HCM — First Industrial 'Closed-Loop' Example in Enterprise Systems
summary: Oracle presented the detailed architecture of Fusion Agentic Applications — AI agents embedded directly into Fusion ERP, HCM, and SCM. Agents execute a full cycle: receive tasks in natural language → analyze data → propose or automatically execute actions (e.g., create a PO, approve leave, adjust inventory forecast) → report results. The key difference from chatbots: agents have access to transactional data and can initiate changes without manual confirmation (with configurable guardrails). Oracle APEX allows building an equivalent agentic architecture for custom business processes outside Fusion — enabling companies without Fusion ERP to achieve a similar level of AI automation.
sourceName: Oracle Fusion Blog

title: SELECT AI in Oracle APEX 24.2: Natural Language to SQL Without Additional Configuration for OCI Databases
summary: Oracle confirmed: SELECT AI is now available as a built-in feature for all Autonomous Database instances on OCI without separate AI profile configuration — simply enable the feature in APEX App Builder. Supported LLMs: OCI Generative AI (Cohere, Llama), OpenAI GPT-4o, Azure OpenAI. For APEX developers: SELECT AI allows adding natural language search to any Interactive Report or Form in minutes without writing LLM integration code.
sourceName: Oracle APEX Documentation

title: APEX 26.1 Early Adopter: What You Can Test Now — APEXlang Compiler Preview, New Chart Types, Improved REST Data Source Wizard
summary: Oracle opened the APEX 26.1 Early Adopter environment for public testing. Available for testing: APEXlang — a declarative language for describing APEX applications in text format (first public compiler preview); new chart types — Gantt Chart and Sankey Diagram as native components; updated REST Data Source wizard with automatic pagination and authentication detection; improved AI Assistant in App Builder — now generates entire pages from a description. For teams planning new APEX projects: Early Adopter lets you evaluate APEXlang as the future version control standard for APEX applications.
sourceName: Oracle APEX Early Adopter

title: Gartner Research: 67% of CEE Enterprises Consider Low-Code the Primary Path for Legacy ERP Migration in 2026–2028
summary: Gartner published the regional study 'ERP Modernization in Central and Eastern Europe 2026'. Key data: 67% of respondents (250+ enterprises across Poland, Czech Republic, Romania, Ukraine, and the Baltic states) named low-code platforms as the primary or alternative path out of legacy ERP; 43% already have active pilots or projects on Oracle APEX, Microsoft Power Apps, or Mendix; the main reason for choosing low-code over traditional ERP: faster time-to-value (median 4–8 months vs. 18–36 for full ERP). APEXREST comment: for companies seeking an alternative to legacy systems, the regional trend confirms that Oracle APEX as a low-code enterprise platform is a proven choice in CEE, not a niche solution.
sourceName: Gartner Research

title: Oracle Database 23ai Free: Free Edition with Full AI Vector Search and JSON Duality Views Now Available for Local Deployment
summary: Oracle updated Oracle Database 23ai Free to version 23.8 — the free edition now includes the full set of AI features without restrictions: Vector Search (VECTOR data type, DBMS_VECTOR package), JSON Relational Duality Views, SQL Domains, True Cache. Limits remain only on resources: 2 CPU threads, 2 GB RAM for DB, 12 GB storage. For teams evaluating a move to Oracle APEX: 23ai Free allows deploying a full APEX + AI Database test environment locally at zero licensing cost — lowering the barrier to entry for pilot projects.
sourceName: Oracle Database Blog

title: BAS (1C Ukraine): Franchisees Confirm Halt of New License Sales to State Sector — Focus Shifts to Supporting Existing Clients
summary: Several major BAS franchisees (Ukrainian 1C localization) confirmed in professional communities that active sales of new licenses to state sector and critical infrastructure clients have been suspended since April 2026. Technical support for existing installations continues until contract expiration. The commercial sector is not yet under a direct ban, but partners report a sharp decline in new agreements due to general uncertainty. APEXREST comment: this is practical confirmation that the 1C/BAS market in Ukraine is entering a contraction phase — the window for an orderly migration to Oracle APEX is now far more comfortable than a forced transition under deadline pressure.
sourceName: DOU.ua — Developer Community

title: Oracle APEX + Generative AI: Step-by-Step LiveLab 'Build a GenAI App in 60 Minutes' — Official Oracle Tutorial for Getting Started with AI in APEX
summary: Oracle published the updated LiveLab 'Build a Generative AI Application with Oracle APEX' with support for APEX 24.2 and OCI Generative AI. Scenario: connect OCI Gen AI as AI provider → configure AI Assistant in APEX App Builder → generate pages and components from a description → integrate SELECT AI into Interactive Report. Completion time: 45–60 minutes on the free OCI Free Tier. For teams evaluating Oracle APEX: the LiveLab is the fastest way to see real AI integration in action without any infrastructure.
sourceName: Oracle LiveLabs

title: Migration from SAP Business One to Oracle APEX: Case Study from a Polish Manufacturing Company — 14 Months, 3 Legal Entities, Full ERP Replacement
summary: A Polish manufacturing company (engineering, ~800 employees) published a detailed case study of migrating from SAP Business One to a custom Oracle APEX application. Key metrics: 14 months from kick-off to go-live across all 3 legal entities; 100% preservation of financial accounting business logic; 3-year TCO — 61% lower compared to continuing with SAP B1 plus required customizations; development team: 3 APEX developers + 1 DBA. APEXREST comment: this case is particularly relevant for companies where SAP B1 is used alongside legacy ERP or as a full ERP system — Oracle APEX demonstrates competitive TCO even relative to the mid-market SAP segment.
sourceName: Oracle Partner Network Blog

title: OCI Cache with Redis (Valkey 8): Oracle Adds JSON Type and ValkeySearch to Managed Cache — New Capabilities for APEX Apps with Vector Similarity Search
summary: Oracle announced GA updates to OCI Cache with Redis: migration to Valkey 8 (open-source Redis 7.2 fork), addition of JSON data type (RedisJSON-compatible API) and ValkeySearch (full-text search + vector similarity search). For Oracle APEX: OCI Cache can now serve as a vector store for RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) scenarios directly in APEX applications without a separate vector database — via REST API with APEX Web Source or the APEX_WEB_SERVICE package. Integration with Oracle AI Database 26ai allows using ValkeySearch as an additional caching layer for frequently queried vector embeddings, reducing load on the primary database and improving AI feature latency in production APEX applications. For teams building AI functionality on APEX: JSON + vector search in managed cache is ready infrastructure for intelligent search without complicating the core infrastructure architecture.
sourceName: Oracle Blog
